title: Melted Middle Eastern Cheese Gratin
description: A briny, crumbly, indulgent Middle Eastern dish featuring cheese slices, binchotan-charcoal, and no onion or garlic, served in a scallop shell.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Place a piece of binchotan charcoal on a heatproof surface and light it with a match or lighter.
Once the charcoal is smoking, place the Halloumi cheese slices on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the olive oil over the cheese and season with salt and black pepper.
Place the baking sheet under the broiler and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Remove the cheese from the oven and place it in scallop shells.
Serve the cheese with pita bread, chopped parsley, and lemon wedges on the side.
Garnish with additional parsley if desired.
